mission.title: Install and Configure Container2wasm on Kubernetes
mission.description: container2wasm is a tool that converts container images into WebAssembly (WASM) format, enabling them to run on WASI runtimes or in the browser. Installing this tool allows you to leverage existing containerized applications in a lightweight, portable format suitable for various environments.

steps.title: Install container2wasm CLI
steps.description:
Download the `c2w` binary from the GitHub releases:
```bash
# macOS (Apple Silicon)
curl -Lo c2w https://github.com/container2wasm/container2wasm/releases/download/v0.6.4/c2w-v0.6.4-linux-amd64
chmod +x c2w
sudo mv c2w /usr/local/bin/

# Linux (amd64)
curl -Lo c2w https://github.com/container2wasm/container2wasm/releases/download/v0.6.4/c2w-v0.6.4-linux-amd64
chmod +x c2w
sudo mv c2w /usr/local/bin/
```
See all binaries at https://github.com/container2wasm/container2wasm/releases

steps.title: Convert a container image to WASM
steps.description:
Convert an Ubuntu container to a WASI image:
```bash
c2w ubuntu:22.04 out.wasm
```
This creates a WASI-compatible image that can run on any WASM runtime.

steps.title: Run the WASM image
steps.description:
Run the converted image using wasmtime or another WASI runtime:
```bash
wasmtime out.wasm uname -a
wasmtime out.wasm ls /
```
You can also map host directories:
```bash
mkdir -p /tmp/share && echo hi > /tmp/share/from-host
wasmtime --mapdir /mnt/share::/tmp/share out.wasm cat /mnt/share/from-host
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that c2w is installed and working:
```bash
c2w --help
c2w --version
```

resolution.summary: container2wasm (c2w) is installed as a CLI tool and can convert container images to WASM format.

upgrade.title: Download the latest release
upgrade.description:
Check for the latest version at https://github.com/container2wasm/container2wasm/releases and download:
```bash
curl -Lo c2w https://github.com/container2wasm/container2wasm/releases/latest/download/c2w-linux-amd64
chmod +x c2w
sudo mv c2w /usr/local/bin/
```

troubleshooting.title: Build fails with memory error
troubleshooting.description:
container2wasm requires significant memory for conversion. Ensure your system has at least 4GB of free RAM:
```bash
free -h
```

troubleshooting.title: WASM runtime not found
troubleshooting.description:
Install a WASI-compatible runtime like wasmtime:
```bash
curl https://wasmtime.dev/install.sh -sSf | bash
```

troubleshooting.title: Slow conversion for large images
troubleshooting.description: Use `--target-arch=riscv64` for riscv64 images which convert faster. x86_64 containers use Bochs emulation which is slower.

prerequisites.description: No Kubernetes cluster required. c2w is a standalone CLI tool. Optionally install a WASI runtime like wasmtime.
